description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun, visually resembling the iconic Thompson M1, often associated with 20th-century urban law enforcement and historical crime settings. It features a robust black metal receiver and barrel assembly, complemented by a dark wooden fixed stock, foregrip, and pistol grip, all with a polished finish. The receiver is detailed with a prominent charging handle and a detachable box magazine, which is centrally located beneath the action. The barrel is ribbed and extends forward, ending in a simple cylindrical muzzle with no visible attachments. Iron sights are mounted on the top, with a rear notch and a front post for aiming. The overall design is utilitarian yet distinctive, with a blend of wood and metal materials that give it a solid, reliable appearance.
description: A classic Thompson-style submachine gun with a black metal body, ribbed barrel, and polished dark wooden stock, foregrip, and pistol grip.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle, ribbed barrel, and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the rifle, wooden stock, foregrip, pistol grip, and detachable box magazine.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the end of the wooden stock and the rear sight alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with the magazine and wooden components visible.
